question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the address book timestamp is fixed at subscription time (addressBookTimestamp captured in AddressBookContext constructor) but the stream can run for a long time via repeatWhen(), causing smart contract clients to receive node data from a stale address book snapshot even after a network topology update has been committed to the database?
